HTML file for a simple Rocky simulator with controls for time and timezone settings.

# Purpose
This HTML file serves as the main interface for the "Simple Rocky Simulator" web application. It includes metadata for character encoding, compatibility, and viewport settings to ensure proper display across different devices and browsers. The file links to external CSS stylesheets for layout and design, and it includes JavaScript files for functionality, such as `rocky.js` and `controls.js`. The HTML structure contains a canvas element for rendering the simulator's graphical output and a form for user input, allowing users to set time, timezone offset, and toggle 24-hour time display. Additionally, the script dynamically loads an application source file based on a query parameter, enabling flexible testing and functionality extension.
# Content Summary
This HTML document is a web page for a "Simple Rocky Simulator." It is structured to include essential metadata and resources for rendering and functionality. The document specifies the HTML5 doctype and includes a copyright notice under the Apache License, Version 2.0.

The `<head>` section contains metadata tags for character set, compatibility, and viewport settings, which are crucial for ensuring proper display across different devices and browsers. It links to external CSS stylesheets, `bootstrap.css` and `style.css`, for styling the page. Additionally, it includes conditional comments to load HTML5 shims and Respond.js for supporting older versions of Internet Explorer (IE8).

The `<body>` section contains a container with a header and two main divisions. The first division includes a `<canvas>` element with the ID "pebble," which is used for rendering the simulator's graphical content. The second division provides a form with controls for user interaction, allowing users to set a time using an epoch timestamp, adjust the timezone offset with a range input, and toggle a 24-hour time display.

The document includes JavaScript to initialize a `RockySimulator` object, binding it to the canvas element. It also defines a function to retrieve query parameters from the URL, which is used to dynamically load additional JavaScript from a specified source. If the `src` query parameter is not empty, it defaults to loading `js/tictoc.js`. The script appends this source as a script element to the HTML document. Finally, an additional script, `js/controls.js`, is included to handle the form controls.
